economic_notes: ITUM has signed Impact and Benefit Agreements (ERA) with several mining companies since 2012. Has a Generations Fund of $18,470,217 accumulated for future generations. Has a reconciliation and collaboration agreement with Rio Tinto-IOC signed October 2020. Holds trust fund (Innu Tshinanu) of $8,500,000 for capital projects. Traditional activities fund of $1,272,501. Enterprises include construction, real estate, fisheries, rail transport, wind energy (APUIAT), and cultural entities.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to non-adoption of SP 3280 (asset retirement obligations) - council did not identify or measure asset retirement liabilities as of April 1, 2022 and March 31, 2023. Financial statements are combined (not consolidated) covering operating fund, housing fund, and enterprises fund. Enterprises (15 entities) are accounted for using modified equity method, not fully consolidated. Participations in enterprises total $22,113,050. Several enterprise financial statements were not available at time of audit (Securite Akua SEC, 9392-8711 Quebec inc., NISHK LP, Centre Culturel Innu). Deferred revenues include large SAC capital project funding. Construction in progress of $9,969,535 is recorded separately. Revenues in advance (revenus percus d'avance) of $10,401,846 noted on balance sheet. Detailed revenue and expense breakdowns by program are in schedules (annexes) not fully extracted here. ISC/SAC funding and total government transfers cannot be precisely disaggregated from the text provided - detailed annexes were referenced but not fully included in the provided text.
